quiz.title: Too Much Water, Too Little Water - Chapter Quiz

questions.questionText: When there is too much rain, it can cause:
questions.explanation: Too much rain causes floods. When rivers overflow or there's heavy continuous rain, water covers areas that are usually dry.
hints: What happens when there's more water than the land can absorb?

questions.questionText: When there is very little or no rain for a long time, it is called:
questions.explanation: A drought occurs when there is very little or no rainfall for an extended period. This causes water shortage and affects crops and drinking water.
We should NEVER play in flood water. It can be dirty, have dangerous animals, or hide deep areas. During floods, move to higher ground and stay safe.

questions.questionText: Which of these is a way to save water?
questions.explanation: Fixing leaking taps saves water. A dripping tap can waste thousands of liters of water in a year!
hints: Which option stops water from being wasted?

questions.explanation: Rainwater harvesting is collecting and storing rainwater for later use. This helps conserve water and increases groundwater levels.

questions.questionText: True or False: In India, some places have floods while others have droughts at the same time.
questions.explanation: True! India is a large country with different weather patterns. Places like Assam may have floods from heavy rain while Rajasthan faces drought at the same time.
hints: Think about how big India is

questions.questionText: What happens to crops during a drought?
questions.explanation: During drought, crops don't get enough water. Without water, plants dry up and die. This affects food production and farmers' income.
hints: What do plants need to survive?

questions.questionText: Which is the best time to water plants?
questions.explanation: The best time to water plants is early morning or evening when it's cool. Less water evaporates, so plants get more water and you save water too!

questions.questionText: During floods, people may have to leave their homes and go to:
questions.explanation: During floods, people often go to relief camps - safe places set up by the government where they get food, water, and shelter.
hints: Where can people get help during disasters?

questions.questionText: How can we reuse water at home?
options: Use water once and throw away
Use washing machine water for plants
questions.explanation: Water from washing clothes (if it doesn't have harsh chemicals) can be used for watering plants. This is called water recycling or reusing.
hints: Can used water still be useful?

questions.explanation: Groundwater is water stored in spaces and cracks under the ground. Wells and bore wells pump this water up. It's an important source of drinking water.

questions.questionText: What should you do to save water while brushing your teeth?
options: Turn off the tap while brushing
questions.explanation: Turn off the tap while brushing your teeth. Only open it when you need to rinse. This simple habit can save many liters of water every day!
hints: Do you need water running while actually brushing?
